records.ety: [L. popularitas an effort to please the people: cf. F. popularit.]

defs: The quality or state of being popular; especially, the state of being esteemed by, or of being in favor with, the people at large; good will or favor proceeding from the people; as, the popularity of a law, statesman, or a book.
quotes: A popularity which has lasted down to our time.

defs: The quality or state of being adapted or pleasing to common, poor, or vulgar people; hence, cheapness; inferiority; vulgarity.
quotes: This gallant laboring to avoid popularity falls into a habit of affectation.

defs: Something which obtains, or is intended to obtain, the favor of the vulgar; claptrap.
quotes: Popularities, and circumstances which . . . sway the ordinary judgment.

defs: The act of courting the favor of the people.

quotes: A little time be allowed for the madness of popularity to cease.
